# Templates with Jinja2

## What You'll Learn
- What Jinja2 is and how it works
- Creating and organizing templates
- Template variables and filters
- Control structures (if, for)
- Template inheritance
- Including templates

## Prerequisites
- Completed Flask Introduction
- Understanding of HTML basics

## What Is Jinja2?

**Jinja2** is Flask's template engine. A template engine lets you create HTML that's partially generated by Python — perfect for dynamic web pages.

Think of templates like cookie cutters:
- The template is the cutter (the shape)
- The data is the dough (the content)
- The result is the cookie (the final HTML)

Jinja2 is built into Flask, so you don't need to install anything extra.

## Setting Up Templates

### Directory Structure

Flask looks for templates in a `templates` folder:

```
my_flask_app/
├── app.py
├── templates/
│   ├── base.html
│   ├── index.html
│   └── about.html
└── static/
    └── style.css
```

### Basic Template Rendering

**app.py:**
```python
from flask import Flask, render_template

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def index() -> str:
    return render_template("index.html")

@app.route("/hello/<name>")
def hello(name: str) -> str:
    return render_template("hello.html", name=name)
```

**templates/index.html:**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Home Page</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Welcome to My Site</h1>
    <p>This page is rendered with Jinja2!</p>
</body>
</html>
```

**templates/hello.html:**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Hello</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Hello, {{ name }}!</h1>
</body>
</html>
```

🔍 **Variable Interpolation:**

1. `{{ name }}` — Double curly braces output the variable's value
2. Flask passes variables from the view function to the template
3. If `name = "Alice"`, it outputs: `<h1>Hello, Alice!</h1>`

## Template Variables

### Passing Variables from Python

```python
@app.route("/profile/<username>")
def profile(username: str) -> str:
    user: dict[str, str | int | bool] = {
        "username": username,
        "name": "Alice Smith",
        "age": 25,
        "is_admin": False,
        "posts": ["Post 1", "Post 2", "Post 3"]
    }
    return render_template("profile.html", user=user, title="User Profile")
```

### Accessing Variables in Templates

```html
<h1>{{ user.username }}</h1>
<p>Name: {{ user.name }}</p>
<p>Age: {{ user.age }}</p>
<p>Is Admin: {{ user.is_admin }}</p>

<!-- Accessing nested data -->
<p>First Post: {{ user.posts[0] }}</p>
```

## Filters

**Filters** modify variables before output. Use the pipe (`|`) character:

```html
<!-- String filters -->
<p>{{ name.upper() }}</p>           <!-- ALICE -->
<p>{{ name.lower() }}</p>           <!-- alice -->
<p>{{ name.title() }}</p>          <!-- Alice -->

<!-- Default value -->
<p>{{ missing_var|default("N/A") }}</p>

<!-- Number formatting -->
<p>{{ 3.14159|round(2) }}</p>      <!-- 3.14 -->

<!-- Length -->
<p>{{ posts|length }}</p>           <!-- 3 -->

<!-- Join -->
<p>{{ tags|join(", ") }}</p>        <!-- python, flask, web -->

<!-- Safe HTML (use carefully!) -->
{{ "<em>italic</em>"|safe }}
```

🔍 **Built-in Filters:**

| Filter | Example | Output |
|--------|---------|--------|
| `upper` | `{{ "hello"|upper }}` | HELLO |
| `lower` | `{{ "HELLO"|lower }}` | hello |
| `title` | | Hello World |
| `default` | `{{ x|default(0) }}` | 0 (if x is undefined) |
| `length` | `{{ list|length }}` | 3 |
| `round` | `{{ 3.7|round }}` | 4 |
| `safe` | | Renders HTML (dangerous!) |

## Control Structures

### If Statements

```html
{% if user.is_admin %}
    <a href="/admin">Admin Panel</a>
{% elif user.is_logged_in %}
    <a href="/dashboard">Dashboard</a>
{% else %}
    <a href="/login">Login</a>
{% endif %}
```

### For Loops

```html
<ul>
{% for post in posts %}
    <li>
        <h2>{{ post.title }}</h2>
        <p>{{ post.content }}</p>
    </li>
{% else %}
    <li>No posts yet.</li>
{% endfor %}
</ul>
```

### Loop Variables

Jinja provides special variables inside loops:

```html
{% for item in items %}
    <!-- Loop.index starts at 1 -->
    <p>{{ loop.index }}. {{ item }}
    
    <!-- First/last iteration -->
    {% if loop.first %}First!{% endif %}
    {% if loop.last %}Last!{% endif %}
    
    <!-- Index starts at 0 -->
    <p>Index: {{ loop.index0 }}</p>
{% endfor %}
```

## Template Inheritance

**Template inheritance** lets you create a base template that other templates extend. This is incredibly powerful for maintaining consistent layouts.

### Base Template

**templates/base.html:**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>{% block title %}My Site{% endblock %}</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ url_for('static', filename='style.css') }}">
</head>
<body>
    <header>
        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('index') }}">Home</a>
            <a href="{{ url_for('about') }}">About</a>
        </nav>
    </header>
    
    <main>
        {% block content %}{% endblock %}
    </main>
    
    <footer>
        <p>&copy; 2024 My Site</p>
    </footer>
</body>
</html>
```

### Child Template

**templates/index.html:**
```html
{% extends "base.html" %}

{% block title %}Home - My Site{% endblock %}

{% block content %}
    <h1>Welcome!</h1>
    <p>This is the home page.</p>
{% endblock %}
```

🔍 **Template Inheritance:**

1. `{% extends "base.html" %}` — Inherits from base template
2. `{% block title %}` — Defines content for a named block
3. `{% block content %}` — Main content area
4. Blocks can be filled or left empty (inherits default)
5. Use `{{ url_for() }}` inside templates for URL generation

### Multiple Levels of Inheritance

```
base.html (contains common structure)
    |
    +-- blog_base.html (adds blog-specific layout)
            |
            +-- blog_post.html (specific page)
```

## Including Templates

**Include** lets you reuse template fragments:

**templates/nav.html:**
```html
<nav>
    <a href="/">Home</a>
    <a href="/about">About</a>
    <a href="/contact">Contact</a>
</nav>
```

**templates/any_page.html:**
```html
<html>
<body>
    {% include "nav.html" %}
    
    <main>
        <h1>Page Content</h1>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

## Macros

**Macros** are reusable template functions:

**templates/macros.html:**
```html
{% macro input_field(name, label, type="text") %}
    <div class="form-group">
        <label for="{{ name }}">{{ label }}</label>
        <input type="{{ type }}" name="{{ name }}" id="{{ name }}">
    </div>
{% endmacro %}

{% macro render_post(post) %}
    <article class="post">
        <h2>{{ post.title }}</h2>
        <p>{{ post.content }}</p>
        <small>By {{ post.author }}</small>
    </article>
{% endmacro %}
```

**Using macros:**
```html
{% from "macros.html" import input_field, render_post %}

<form>
    {{ input_field("username", "Username") }}
    {{ input_field("email", "Email", "email") }}
    {{ input_field("password", "Password", "password") }}
</form>

{% for post in posts %}
    {{ render_post(post) }}
{% endfor %}
```

## Complete Example

### app.py

```python
from flask import Flask, render_template

app = Flask(__name__)

posts: list[dict[str, str | int]] = [
    {"id": 1, "title": "First Post", "content": "Hello World!", "author": "Alice"},
    {"id": 2, "title": "Second Post", "content": "Flask is fun!", "author": "Bob"},
]

@app.route("/")
def index() -> str:
    return render_template("index.html", posts=posts)

@app.route("/post/<int:post_id>")
def post(post_id: int) -> str:
    post_data: dict | None = next((p for p in posts if p["id"] == post_id), None)
    if post_data is None:
        return "Post not found", 404
    return render_template("post.html", post=post_data)
```

### templates/base.html

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>{% block title %}My Blog{% endblock %}</title>
    <style>
        body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 20px; }
        header { border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; padding-bottom: 10px; margin-bottom: 20px; }
        nav a { margin-right: 15px; }
        article { margin-bottom: 30px; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <header>
        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('index') }}">Home</a>
        </nav>
    </header>
    
    <main>
        {% block content %}{% endblock %}
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

### templates/index.html

```html
{% extends "base.html" %}

{% block title %}Home - My Blog{% endblock %}

{% block content %}
    <h1>Blog Posts</h1>
    
    {% for post in posts %}
        <article>
            <h2><a href="{{ url_for('post', post_id=post.id) }}">{{ post.title }}</a></h2>
            <p>By {{ post.author }}</p>
            <p>{{ post.content }}</p>
        </article>
    {% else %}
        <p>No posts yet!</p>
    {% endfor %}
{% endblock %}
```

### templates/post.html

```html
{% extends "base.html" %}

{% block title %}{{ post.title }} - My Blog{% endblock %}

{% block content %}
    <article>
        <h1>{{ post.title }}</h1>
        <p><em>By {{ post.author }}</em></p>
        <div>{{ post.content }}</div>
        
        <p><a href="{{ url_for('index') }}">&larr; Back to posts</a></p>
    </article>
{% endblock %}
```

## Summary
- **Jinja2** is Flask's built-in template engine
- Use `{{ variable }}` to output variables
- Use `{% tag %}` for control structures (if, for)
- Use **filters** (`| upper`, `| default`) to modify output
- Use **inheritance** (`{% extends %}`) for consistent layouts
- Use **blocks** (`{% block %}`) for customizable areas
- Use **macros** for reusable template functions
- Use `{{ url_for() }}` for generating URLs

→ Continue to `04-forms-and-user-input.md` to learn how to handle form submissions and user input.
